title: tranquil briny ukrainian fernbrake-gosari stew
description: a hearty, nostalgic stew from ukraine featuring fernbrake-gosari and a rich, briny broth.
instructions: cut the fernbrake-gosari into bite-sized pieces and set aside.
cook the bacon in a large cazuela over medium heat until crispy, then remove from heat and set aside.
add the smoked paprika, black pepper, and kosher salt to the cazuela and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
add the apple cider vinegar to the cazuela and cook until almost completely reduced, stirring occasionally.
add the worcestershire sauce to the cazuela and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
add the chicken or vegetable broth to the cazuela and bring to a boil.
add the fernbrake-gosari to the cazuela and cook until tender, about 20 minutes.
stir in the cooked bacon and capers.
season the stew with salt and pepper to taste.
serve the stew hot, topped with a dollop of sour cream and some chopped fresh parsley.
deglaze the cazuela with a little water and serve with the stew.
